evals.prompt: can you get opencode to review my jwt refactor? i just rewrote the token validation in src/auth/jwt.ts and want a second set of eyes before i push. send it to pane %3
evals.expected_output: Agent reads the local-markdown format file from resources/formats/local-markdown.md, writes a request file to the reviews/ directory with correct YAML front matter (agent, model, working_dir, timestamp, prompt fields), includes ## What to do and ## Context sections, and uses the reviewer prompt template from the format file to send to pane %3 via tmux.
expectations: A request file was written to the reviews/ directory with a session name like YYYYMMDD-jwt-refactor-request.md
The request file contains YAML front matter with at least: agent, model, working_dir, timestamp, prompt fields
The request file contains a ## What to do section
The request file contains a ## Context section
The format file at resources/formats/local-markdown.md was read before writing
The reviewer prompt sent via tmux includes the absolute path to the request file

evals.prompt: have kiro check my rate limiter — target is main:kiro.0. specifically i want to know if it'll hold up under concurrent load. the impl is in src/middleware/rate-limit.ts
evals.expected_output: Agent writes a request file with the focus field set to the concurrent load question, includes the specific question prominently in the ## Context section, and sends the reviewer prompt to main:kiro.0.
expectations: A request file was written to the reviews/ directory
The request file YAML front matter includes a focus field mentioning concurrent requests or concurrent load
The ## Context section references rate-limit.ts or the rate limiter
The tmux send-keys command targets main:kiro.0

evals.prompt: clean up the peer review files
evals.expected_output: Agent lists all .md files in the reviews/ directory, groups them by session prefix, and asks the user which ones to remove — without deleting anything until confirmed.
expectations: Agent runs ls or equivalent to list files in the reviews/ directory
Agent presents the list of sessions to the user
Agent does NOT delete any files without user confirmation
Agent asks which sessions to remove
